suppressor_alien_mk1_master.notes: Baseline heavy suppressor with broad armored shell and oppressive frontal mass.

suppressor_alien_elite.notes: Larger command-feeling unit with heavier plating and stronger red-orange core presence.

suppressor_alien_artillery.notes: Elongated siege organism with frontal bombardment structure and stable heavy geometry.

suppressor_alien_brute.notes: Extremely wide tank-like alien organism with massive plating and crushing silhouette.

readabilityNotes: Suppressor units are defined by broad armored mass, red-orange alien energy, thick biomechanical plating, and an oppressive frontal silhouette.
The faction contrasts strongly with Alien Scout: Suppressors are wider, heavier, warmer in palette, and less agile-looking.
Large shell panels and central red-orange cores preserve readability behind dense bullet patterns.
Variant silhouettes are distinct at 64x64: mk1 is baseline, elite is denser and higher-rank, artillery is elongated, and brute is the widest pressure unit.
androidReadabilityNotes: Recommended display size is 64x64 or larger due to the heavy silhouette language.
The red-orange core and broad armor blocks remain readable on small mobile displays.
Avoid excessive overlap for brute and elite formations because their wide silhouettes can visually merge.
silhouetteNotes: Faction identity comes from rectangular mass, thick shell plating, red-orange energy seams, and industrial-organic siege structure.
Elite should read as a command-heavy variant through increased armor density.
Artillery should read as a ranged bombardment threat through its longer front structure.
Brute should read as a tank-like pressure enemy through maximum width and side armor mass.
integrationNotes: Asset preparation only; no gameplay, draw, runtime logic, collisions, hitboxes, AI, rank, or balance changes were made.
Sheet frame order is mk1_master, elite, artillery, brute.
